headline: A low-skilled attacker wielding Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex breached 14 companies, the clearest sign yet that agentic AI is collapsing the skill floor for cyberattacks.

emerging_trends.text: Off-the-shelf coding agents like Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex now let novices run intrusions that once demanded years of skill, and defenders concede capable hacking models will spread no matter what export controls say.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Agentic AI Collapses the Attacker Skill Floor

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep seizing trusted package maintainers, this week hijacking one npm account to poison 144 Mastra AI-framework libraries that thousands of developers pull blind.

Cryptocurrency thieves dress malware in legitimacy through paid news posts, fake VirusTotal comments, and AI-narrated videos to push a worm-like clipper that quietly swaps wallet addresses at the moment of a transfer.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Crypto Clipper Spreads Through Fake Hype

topics.title: FortiBleed Credential Leak Widens
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer pinned the leak at 73,932 Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credential URLs, and SOCRadar counted 30,000 compromised firewalls exposed through three recently patched FortiSandbox flaws.
topics.summary: The FortiBleed dump exposes working VPN credentials for tens of thousands of Fortinet devices at organizations across nearly 200 countries, among them Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, and a NATO contractor. Rotate credentials and patch FortiSandbox at once.

sources.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs
Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices

topics.title: Surveillance and Privacy Rules Shift
topics.latest_developments: A $10 million procurement reviewed by 404 Media shows ICE buying immigrants' tax identifiers from a data broker, which Senator Ron Wyden calls an end-run around a court order, as Google prepares to use UK and EU IP addresses for ad personalization from August 3 and Britain readies a social-media ban for under-16s.
topics.summary: Governments and platforms are widening data collection on citizens, spanning immigration enforcement, ad targeting, and age verification. Privacy experts warn each move erodes anonymity and expands the surveillance surface.

sources.title: ICE Appears to Be Buying Immigrants' Tax Identifiers from a Data Broker
Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization
UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried

topics.title: Agentic AI Collapses the Attacker Skill Floor
topics.latest_developments: OALABS recovered more than 1,000 agent sessions from a compromised server and found a low-skilled attacker ran Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex to slip past their guardrails and breach 14 companies.
topics.summary: Researchers warn agentic coding tools hand novices the reach of seasoned intruders, and Ars Technica adds that models with strong hacking ability will become common whatever export rules say. Treat AI agents as a live offensive capability and tighten monitoring of their use.

sources.title: Low-skilled attacker used Claude, Codex to breach 14 companies
"Dangerous" AI models are coming no matter what

topics.title: Secure Boot and BitLocker Defenses Falter
topics.latest_developments: Ars Technica warned that Secure Boot signing keys on Windows and Linux machines expire soon and need replacing, while the leaker Nightmare Eclipse dropped three Microsoft zero-days, one letting anyone with a USB stick walk past BitLocker disk encryption.
topics.summary: Expiring Secure Boot keys threaten to break trusted boot, and the BitLocker bypass undermines full-disk encryption on lost or stolen laptops. Update Secure Boot keys before the deadline and watch for Microsoft's emergency fixes.

sources.title: Windows and Linux users: The deadline to update Secure Boot keys is near
Smashing Security podcast #472: AI gets hacked, and BitLocker gets bypassed

topics.title: Crypto Clipper Spreads Through Fake Hype
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Check Point Research detailed a clipboard-hijacking clipper that swaps cryptocurrency wallet addresses, spreads worm-like, reaches Tor command servers, and builds buzz through paid news posts, fake VirusTotal comments, and AI-narrated YouTube videos.
topics.summary: The clipper replaces a copied wallet address with the attacker's the moment a victim pastes it, diverting transfers, and plants a lightweight backdoor for follow-on access. Verify pasted crypto addresses and shun software promoted through unsolicited reviews.

sources.title: Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments

topics.title: Mastra npm Packages Hijacked
topics.latest_developments: Attackers hijacked the npm account ehindero to compromise 144 packages in the Mastra AI-framework namespace, a campaign Endor Labs, JFrog, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity codenamed easy-day-js.
topics.summary: Mastra is a widely used open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building AI applications, so the poisoned @mastra/* packages endanger every project that pulls them. Pin versions, audit installs, and rotate any exposed tokens.

sources.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account

weather: Overnight: Scattered Showers And Thunderstorms, low 68F.
Thursday: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Mostly Sunny, high 84F.

games.result: Pirates 12 · Athletics 3 · Bot 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: U.S. and Iran Sign War-Ending Accord
business_politics.latest_developments: The United States and Iran signed the memorandum of understanding Wednesday, days ahead of the Friday ceremony planned in Switzerland, and Pakistan's prime minister said it took immediate effect.
business_politics.summary: The accord winds down the war that began February 28, releases Iran's frozen funds, and eases banking and transport sanctions while letting Tehran keep its ballistic missiles; the Wall Street Journal estimates the restored oil sales could earn Iran more than $60 billion a year, and President Trump warned he could resume bombing if Tehran breaks the terms.

sources.title: US and Iran sign deal as Trump vows to release frozen funds and ease sanctions
Deal Gives Iran Chance to Turbocharge Its Oil Revenue

business_politics.title: Warsh Fed Signals a Rate Rise
business_politics.latest_developments: At Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chair the Federal Open Market Committee held its benchmark rate steady in a unanimous vote, and officials' new projections pointed to at least one increase by year-end, sending stocks lower and Treasury yields and the dollar higher.
business_politics.summary: Warsh, who scrapped the Fed's explicit forward guidance and dropped its bias toward cuts, framed the hawkish turn as taming the inflation jolt from the Iran war, which has pushed prices toward double the central bank's 2% target.

sources.title: Fed officials tilt towards rate rise as Kevin Warsh era begins
Warsh's Commitment to Inflation Fight Sparks Market Slide
Federal Reserve holds rates with short statement and hawkish projections

business.title: Skill-Games Taxation in Limbo
business.latest_developments: Pennsylvania operators, lawmakers, and players are weighing how the machines will be taxed after this week's state Supreme Court ruling classified skill games as slot machines under state law.
business.summary: The decision subjects the thousands of skill games in Pennsylvania bars, convenience stores, and clubs to gaming regulation, and Harrisburg now faces pressure to set a tax rate on a market that has run untaxed for years.

sources.title: Pennsylvanians weigh court ruling that could lead to taxation on skill games

business.title: Pittsburgh Limits Vape Shops by Zoning
business.latest_developments: Mayor Corey O'Connor signed an ordinance Wednesday that uses zoning rules to restrict where vape shops can operate in Pittsburgh.
business.summary: The legislation sets zoning limits on vape and tobacco retailers across the city, the latest municipal effort to curb their spread.

sources.title: Pittsburgh mayor signs vape shop zoning ordinance

around_town.title: Storms, Tornado Threat Hit Region Thursday Morning
around_town.latest_developments: Storms moved into Western Pennsylvania overnight into Thursday, June 18, carrying a tornado risk, flash flooding, and damaging winds, and Duquesne Light urged customers to ready for outages.
around_town.summary: The National Weather Service flagged the early-Thursday system as a severe-weather threat for the Pittsburgh region, with strong low-level wind shear keeping storms organized through the morning commute before gusty winds linger most of the day.

sources.title: Severe Weather Alert Day for rain, wind on Thursday AM
Strong storms to move through Western Pennsylvania overnight
Duquesne Light alerts residents ahead of anticipated severe storms in Western Pa.

around_town.title: Commercial Street to Close June 29
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT moved up the closure under the Commercial Street Bridge on the Parkway East to June 29, earlier than the early-July start it first announced, ahead of a full bridge closure in July.
around_town.summary: The work on Interstate 376 in Pittsburgh's East End will shut Commercial Street beneath the span, a change that has drawn complaints from nearby residents.

sources.title: Commercial Street to close on June 29 ahead of Parkway East bridge closure in July

around_town.title: Pittsburgh Relaunches Police Co-Response Teams
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh's Office of Community Health and Safety relaunched and moved to expand its co-response program Wednesday, pairing a police officer with a social worker on certain 911 calls.
around_town.summary: Community social worker Jaime Gribben-Mahoney and her police partners answer mental-health and related calls together in Downtown Pittsburgh, an approach the city says reaches people reluctant to deal with officers alone.

sources.title: Pittsburgh relaunches OCHS Co-Response Program for mental health emergencies
Pittsburgh plans to expand co-response program, pairing police with mental health clinicians

events.title: Juneteenth Observances Across Pittsburgh
events.latest_developments: Pittsburgh's Juneteenth festivals, film screenings, and gatherings run up to the June 19 holiday, now two days out.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette's guide rounds up the region's June 19 observances, from festivals to film screenings marking the end of slavery in the United States.

sources.title: Juneteenth events in Pittsburgh include festivals, films and more

around_teams.title: Steelers Likely to Pass on QB Brendan Sorsby
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette writers said this week the Steelers should and likely will sit out any bid for quarterback Brendan Sorsby in the NFL supplemental draft.
around_teams.summary: Sorsby became available through the supplemental draft after a gambling matter, and the Post-Gazette's analysis concluded the Steelers have little reason to spend a pick to acquire him.

sources.title: WATCH: Why the Steelers would be right to avoid Brendan Sorsby
Don't expect the Steelers to make a bid for Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft

around_teams.title: Film Room: Rookie DT Gabriel Rubio
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette's film breakdown cast Steelers defensive tackle Gabriel Rubio, from Notre Dame, as a solid run defender whose violent hands hold up at the point of attack.
around_teams.summary: Rubio projects as a rotational run-stopper on a Steelers defensive line that includes Derrick Harmon.

sources.title: Steelers film room: Gabriel Rubio's violent hands make him a solid run defender

around_teams.title: Ex-Steel City Infielders Return With Athletics
around_teams.latest_developments: Jacob Wilson and Alika Williams, in town with the Athletics for the series against the Pirates, told the Post-Gazette they hold fond memories of their time in Pittsburgh.
around_teams.summary: The two Athletics infielders, with Pittsburgh roots, reflected on their days in the city as the clubs met this week at PNC Park.

sources.title: Now with the Athletics, Jacob Wilson and Alika Williams relish their Pittsburgh memories

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: The State of Fable, The Jailbreak Problem, SpaceX Acquires Cursor
reading.summary: Argues the Trump administration is very likely wrong about Anthropic's Fable model, and that responsibility for the jailbreak problem ultimately rests with Anthropic itself.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Reports that OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, building the case that the company has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responds to a Financial Times interview with Rebecca Hinds of the Work AI Institute about a survey of 6,000 workers, arguing the dysfunction people blame on AI was already built into modern knowledge work.
